tldr: Stark embeds accessibility checks inside design tools like Figma, while A11yWatch is an open-source automated WCAG scanner for shipped code. Switching means moving accessibility checks from design-time to runtime CI scans.
why_switch: Need automated runtime WCAG checks on production
Integrate a11y into CI and PR pipelines
Catch issues introduced in code, not just design
what_to_export: Contrast and focus-order reports per design
Tickets filed from Stark into your tracker
export_method: Stark's findings live alongside Figma designs and inside the Stark workspace. Open each design file and document any annotations, contrast reports or focus-order notes Stark added; copy them into your shared drive or design system documentation. Use the Stark dashboard's export options where available to save audit summaries. Designs themselves stay in Figma. Tickets you filed into Jira, Linear or GitHub remain in your tracker. Treat the design files plus exported reports as the migration baseline.
import_method: A11yWatch is a runtime scanner, so the import step is configuration rather than data load. Self-host A11yWatch or use the hosted tier and point scans at your staging and production URLs. Configure scans via the CLI, API or GitHub Action and integrate with PR checks. Use your archived Stark contrast and focus-order findings as a baseline to confirm the issues are caught at runtime. Keep design-time checks separately if your designers still want them; A11yWatch does not replace Figma plugins.
gotchas: Stark and A11yWatch sit at different stages: design-time vs runtime; you may want both in parallel
Automated rules typically miss a meaningful share of real a11y issues
Self-hosting A11yWatch means owning uptime and upgrades
Designers may still need Figma-native checks; consider keeping a small Stark seat count
Reporting formats and severity scales will differ

email.subject: Moving accessibility checks from Stark to A11yWatch
email.body:
Hi team,

Over the next 14 days we will be shifting our accessibility tooling from Stark to A11yWatch. Stark has helped designers catch issues in Figma, but we now need automated WCAG checks on shipped code, integrated into CI, which is what A11yWatch provides.

Plan:
- Days 1-3: archive Stark annotations and audit reports into our design system documentation.
- Days 4-7: stand up A11yWatch and run a baseline scan on staging and production.
- Days 8-12: wire A11yWatch into PR checks and scheduled crawls.
- Days 13-14: finalize the cutover and reduce Stark seats to design-only usage.

We may keep a small number of Stark seats for designers since the two tools cover different stages of the pipeline.

Thanks,
Your customer
